channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate passes Social Security benefits boost for many public service retirees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed legislation early Saturday to boost Social Security payments for millions of people, pushing a longtime priority for former public employees through Congress in one of its last acts for the year.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden considers commuting the sentences of federal death row inmates: report
item.description: President Biden is reportedly considering commuting the sentences of most, if not all, of the 40 men on the federal government’s death row.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate approves 235th judge of Biden's term, beating Trump's tally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden secured the 235th judicial confirmation of his presidency Friday, an accomplishment that exceeds his predecessor's total by one after Democrats put extra emphasis on the federal courts following Donald Trump's far-reaching first term, when he filled three seats
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Texas coal-fired power plant to switch to clean energy after receiving more than $1 billion in federal money
item.description: San Miguel Electric Cooperative's plan to turn into a solar and battery plant will leave only 14 coal-fired power plants in the state.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Juan Salinas Ii, KPRC 2

item.title: A judge says Missouri's abortion ban isn’t enforceable, but there's no start date for abortions
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A judge ruled Friday that Missouri’s near-total abortion ban is unenforceable under a new constitutional amendment, though Planned Parenthood said the decision is still not sufficient for it to resume providing abortions in the state.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Valadao, Fong vote for government funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: Reps. David Valadao and Vince Fong both voted for a government funding bill that passed the House late Friday afternoon just hours before a shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: Schools are also warning that international students should travel with additional documentation of their connection to their American college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Adams says Dems 'missed the memo' on migrant crisis and it hurt the party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams sat down with PIX on Politics host Dan Mannarino for an exclusive year-end interview, looking back at what was undoubtedly a very eventful 2024 and ahead to what’s in store for 2025.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: The 34 House Republicans who voted against a bill to avert a partial government shutdown
item.description: Thirty-four House Republicans voted Friday against a spending bill to avoid a partial government shutdown, while no Democrats opposed the legislation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden signs bill to avert government shutdown
item.description: After weeks of back and forth, the House and Senate have agreed on a spending deal to avoid a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: What Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Rising threats are driving local officials out of office, new California study shows
item.description: There has been a rise in threats made against local elected officials and candidates, prompting some to consider leaving their post or change their stance on a specific issue due to a 'climate of fear,' according to a recent study out of the University of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota House tie in peril after judge rules DFL lawmaker can’t be sworn in
item.description: A district court judge on Friday granted a challenge to Democratic Rep.-elect Curtis Johnson’s residency, blocking him from taking office.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost about 2.1 million troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday's deadline to prevent a government shutdown, the Pentagon warned.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans scramble for funding plan C as shutdown deadline draws near
item.description: Congress is racing toward a shutdown at the end of the day Friday, and Republicans appear no closer to finding a path forward that will keep the lights on and appease President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Luna wants disaster aid separate from government funding bill
item.description: Hurricane survivors will need to wait a while longer to see additional disaster relief aid come from Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Shutdown odds rapidly rise as House seeks plan B
item.description: The odds of a shutdown were rapidly growing on Thursday after President-elect Trump upended the talks by urging GOP lawmakers to shutter the government unless Democrats agree to a clean stopgap spending bill that raises or abolishes the debt limit.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Dems say 'hell no' to GOP plan B on government funding, debt ceiling
item.description: House Democrats are rallying in overwhelming opposition to the Republicans' second stab at a government funding bill, vowing to sink it on the House floor and force GOP leaders back to the table to negotiate a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump backs plan B spending deal, urges swift passage
item.description: “All Republicans, and even the Democrats, should do what is best for our Country, and vote ‘YES’ for this Bill, TONIGHT!“ Trump posted on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: FAA bans drones in parts of New Jersey
item.description: The FAA warned that "deadly force" could be used against the drones if they pose an "imminent security threat.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Party switch paying off? Valdes secures committee leadership after joining GOP
item.description: One state Representative in the Tampa Bay area made the controversial move to switch political parties, just last week. The news came was greeted with both cheers and criticisms.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Ethics Committee secretly voted to release Matt Gaetz ethics report, source says
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has voted in secret to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Federal Reserve cuts its key rate by a quarter-point but envisions fewer reductions next year
item.description: The Federal Reserve cut its key interest rate by a quarter-point — its third cut this year — but also signaled that it expects to reduce rates more slowly next year than it previously envisioned, largely because of still-elevated inflation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gaetz defends ‘embarrassing’ past behavior, fires shot at Ethics report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) is defending what he says is "embarrassing, though not criminal" past behavior and taking a shot at the House Ethics Committee, following reports that the panel voted to release its findings about him.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota-backed bill to make bald eagles the national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: The bald eagle could soon become the national bird of the United States after a bill backed by Minnesota legislators passed the U.S. House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump weighs in on NYC mayor, vaccines and drones in freewheeling press conference at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump told reporters that he would consider pardoning embattled New York Mayor Eric Adams, said the country was “not going to lose” the polio vaccine and weighed in on the flurry of drone sightings over New Jersey during a freewheeling press conference Monday at his Mar-a-Lago
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump girds for battle with Democrats, Supreme Court over birthright citizenship
item.description: President-elect Trump, Democratic lawmakers and the Supreme Court are on a collision course over restricting birthright citizenship, which Trump has identified as a top priority for his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5
